comment.body:
Curious why folks are picking puts for next week since Alma's post today morning said that window for pullback is shifting to the post Sept Opex/FOMC window and bearish trend is not seen. Am I missing something ?

"So the probability of malign outcomes of this August risk window eases, because debt burden must be managed first, and it is shifting towards the post-Sept OpEx/FOMC window, especially because a slow sell-off would ease the wealth-effect caused inflationary pressures, but stay cautious!
No long-term positions for me. A market correction is timely, prices are temporaly high. But I don’t see bearish trend now, only high odds for a pullback."
comment.raw_body:
